results.rendered_html: bhartṛ—hari m. N. of a well-known poet and grammarian (of the 7th century A.D.; author of 300 moral, political, and religious maxims comprised in 3 Śatakas, and of the Vākyapadīya and other gram. works., and according to some also of the Bhaṭṭi-kāvya)

results.rendered_html:
bhartṛhari (bhartar + hari) m. N. pr. eines Bruders des Königs Vikramāditya, dem die Autorschaft dreier Śataka (Śṛṅgāra-Ś., Nīti-Ś. und Vairāgya-Ś.), grammatischer Kārikā, des Vākyapadīya und von Einigen auch des Bhaṭṭikāvya zugeschrieben wird,  LIA. II, 803 . 1161. fgg.   TRIK. 2,7,26 .  Verz. d. Oxf. H. 161,b,8 . 160,b,8 . 160,b,3 . 175,b (No. 398). 177,b,8 . 239,a,9 .  SĀH. D. 32,3 .  WASSILJEW 54 .
— Vgl. bhartṛhema und hari.

results.rendered_html: bhartṛhari Author of the three Śatakas and of the Vākyapadīya. Mr. Telang gives it as his opinion that he must have flourished about the close of the first and the beginning of the second century of the Christian era. Tradition makes him brother of king Vikrama, and if this Vikrama be accepted as the same who defeated the Mlecchas in 544 A. D., we must suppose Bhartṛhari to have flourished in the latter half of the sixth century.
